item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here and requires purchase for official use. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: Dogs don't have immortal souls
item.content:encoded: If this urn is supposed to be Catholic, then Addendum 2997-01 should be altered or removed, as it shouldn't show dogs. While I can't find an official document of the Church confirming this information, orthodox Catholic doctrine teaches that the souls of animals are mortal, and therefore die with their bodies, as proven in Saint Thomas Aquinas's Summa Contra Gentiles, Book 2, Chapter 82.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The "engraving that reveals the object's purpose" thing usually feels hokey to me, and being done twice here, as if the author couldn't decide between the two, was especially shaky.

But other than that, it was a simple, enjoyable article that hit its mark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uuhhhhh, I never actually thought about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does a tracking chip work on this thing? You can't track a radiation absorber with electromagnetic waves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason, nuclear shadows make me squick far more than any amount of gore. Probably something to do with the whole vaporization thing. Blurry silhouette of someone who was struck by the nuclear delete key. *shudders*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The entire part about the cathedral and the people in mass is historically accurate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That they were in mass at the time of the explosion (although I want to say that Nagasaki's bombing was on a Sunday) feels like an accurate prediction of the end of their lives, and that connects the two with a sentiment that I like.

Edit: It was a Thursday (August 9th, 1945 (Hiroshima was three days earlier, on Monday, August 6th)), although I don't know that that would actually be unusual for Eastern or specifically Japanese worship. Any people with cultural insight please tell me if I'm ignorant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the Christian element of it seems more legitimate than many of the peer articles with the same take; if anything, this feels more original to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said in chat, I'm not really seeing why Christianity has to be here, as it clashes thematically with the subject material you've chosen. I also don't much care for the impact of the article on the reader relying pretty much entirely on the events of Nagasaki, rather than anything of genuine interest in the article itself. It's improved from the earlier versions I saw, but this still really doesn't do it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"has ranged from extremely dangerous to barely measurable."

Might sound better as "ranges from lethal to infinitesimal." with respects to sciencifying it up a tad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the issue that I had with it. We have far too many Abrahamic SCP's, and this one seemed a little more contrived to fit that in, especially in a scenario like Japan.

It was well-written enough to avoid the downvote though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you should mention that it doesn't release it's gamma waves in the description.

As for the article: I don't know, something about it bothers me. I know that Nagasaki had and still has a Christian population…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the idea. It's self containing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm assuming 2997-1's gamma radiation doesn't leave 2997? Because I'm pretty sure we'd need a bit more in the containment procedures if it does release gamma rays.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so amazingly beautiful and simple. Thank you.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
praying at the shrine containing SCP-2997 at the time of.

I guess this is time of containment but not 100% so won't fill it in. I'm having a bit of trouble figuring out how they can work out what the shadows are doing, I can't imagine figuring out that a shadow is treating burns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
I want to thank Djoric for helping me flesh out this idea a lot, Quikgruvn for coming up with the title for this, thedeadlymoose, chubert, and Kalinin for giving very detailed reviews of this, and anyone else who reviewed this in chat or on the forums.

Hope you enjoyed the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are two Japaneses inscriptions on SCP-2997 that read, "Remember that you are dust, and to dust you shall return." and, "They believed."

You mean 2 Japanese or 2 Japanese's?
